key_insight: Frontier LLMs are already performing strongly on business-case analytical reasoning, and their ability on this kind of knowledge work is improving quickly.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark shows how to evaluate AI on messy, real-world decision-making tasks that require synthesis, judgment, and trade-off analysis rather than only narrow math or QA skills.
method: Built BusinessCaseBench from hundreds of questions drawn from business cases across 18 disciplines.
Paired each question with a grading rubric derived from the expert instructor case solution.
Evaluated frontier models on case-based analytical reasoning and tracked improvement within a model family over two years.
evidence_signal: The benchmark uses case-derived questions and instructor rubrics, and the abstract reports that frontier models score highly with substantial gains over two years in one model family.
watch_item: Case-rubric scoring may still miss nuanced judgment, subjective reasoning, and real-world context that human experts use when evaluating open-ended analyses.
paper_title: Frontier AI performance across the business disciplines: a case-grounded benchmark of knowledge work and analytical reasoning
